name: statewave-ide-companion
description: A trustworthy project brain for your AI assistant. Statewave makes Copilot/Cursor/Claude Code aware of your workspace, docs, git history, code structure and conventions over MCP — local, deterministic, zero-config. It never reads your assistant chat.
author: Statewave <hello@statewave.ai>

untrustedWorkspaces.description: In untrusted workspaces Statewave does not ingest, wire MCP, or write instruction files. Preview and configuration still work.

virtualWorkspaces.description: Git/symbol/scan features require files on disk; MCP wiring still works.

walkthroughs.title: Get started with Statewave
walkthroughs.description: Give your AI assistant a trustworthy project brain — local, private, deterministic.

steps.title: What Statewave does (and doesn't)
steps.description:
Statewave makes your assistant aware of this project's structure, docs, git history and conventions — read back over MCP. It never reads your chat and never ingests until you ask.
[Privacy & redaction](command:statewave.showIndexedFiles)

steps.title: Connect your Statewave server
steps.description:
Point the plugin at your Statewave instance (defaults to http://localhost:8100). One setting — MCP wiring is automatic.
[Configure Statewave](command:statewave.configureStatewave)

steps.title: See exactly what will be indexed
steps.description:
Transparency first — review what's indexed and what's skipped (secrets are always skipped).
[Show Indexed Files](command:statewave.showIndexedFiles)

steps.title: Build project memory (preview-first)
steps.description:
Runs a preview; nothing is sent until you press Ingest.
[Build Project Memory](command:statewave.buildProjectMemory)

steps.title: Ask your assistant
steps.description:
Open the project understanding, then ask your assistant to use the Statewave memory.
[Open Project Understanding](command:statewave.openProjectUnderstanding)

statewave.url.markdownDescription: Base URL of your Statewave instance. Defaults to `http://localhost:8100` so a local instance needs zero setup. Set to empty to force preview-only (never ingest). Ingestion still always requires the explicit “Ingest to Statewave” action — a URL alone never sends anything.

statewave.apiKey.markdownDescription: Statewave API key. **Prefer setting this in your user/machine settings, not committed workspace settings.** It is never logged or sent anywhere except your configured `statewave.url`.

enumDescriptions: Use repo:<owner>/<repo> when a git remote is detected, otherwise workspace:<folder>
Always repo:<owner>/<repo> (requires a parseable git remote)
statewave.subjectStrategy.markdownDescription: How the Statewave subject for this workspace is derived. Override entirely with `statewave.subject`.

statewave.subject.markdownDescription: Explicit subject override. When non-empty, used verbatim and `statewave.subjectStrategy` is ignored.

statewave.autoIndex.markdownDescription: When enabled, the file watcher may send `ide.file.changed` episodes automatically as you save. **Off by default — nothing is ever ingested without an explicit command or this opt-in.**

statewave.includeGlobs.markdownDescription: Extra globs to include. A match here force-includes a path even if it sits under a default-ignored directory (e.g. `dist/keep/**`).

statewave.excludeGlobs.markdownDescription: Globs to exclude, in addition to the built-in ignore set (node_modules, .git, dist, build, coverage, lockfiles, …).

statewave.redaction.enabled.markdownDescription: Apply best-effort email / phone / API-key redaction to every episode's text before it leaves the editor. On by default.

statewave.compileAfterIngest.markdownDescription: After a successful ingest, compile the workspace subject into durable memory so Copilot/Cursor can retrieve it immediately. On by default. Turn off to ingest episodes without triggering the heavier compile pass.

statewave.mcp.autoWire.markdownDescription: Automatically make the Statewave MCP server available to the assistant from this one setting block — no separate MCP config file to edit, no container to run. Each client is only touched when it is actually installed; secrets only ever land in home-dir/editor-storage files, never in the repo. Turn off to wire MCP yourself.

enumDescriptions: Tell the assistant to consult Statewave before answering AND persist durable facts you state (e.g. 'my favorite color is red') via the MCP tool
Tell the assistant to consult Statewave before answering project questions; do not persist chat facts
statewave.assistantInstructions.markdownDescription: Writes a small, no-secret rules file per detected assistant so it **reflexively** uses the project brain. The plugin never reads your chat — in `read-write` the *assistant* persists durable facts you state by calling the `statewave_ingest_episode` MCP tool (visible/approvable). These files belong in the repo and are safe to commit.

statewave.mcp.clients.markdownDescription: Which assistant clients to auto-wire. `copilot` = in-memory VS Code provider; `cursor` = `~/.cursor/mcp.json`; `windsurf` = `~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json`; `claude` = `~/.claude.json` (local scope); `cline`/`roo` = their editor globalStorage settings; `continue` = `~/.continue/config.yaml`; `codex` = `~/.codex/config.toml` (`[mcp_servers.statewave]`). Remove an entry to skip that client.

statewave.github.enabled.markdownDescription: Optional **opt-in** GitHub history connector. When on, **Statewave: Sync GitHub Project History** pulls issues / PRs / comments / reviews / releases via [`@statewavedev/connectors-github`](https://github.com/smaramwbc/statewave-connectors) and ingests them under this workspace's subject — captures the *“why”* the IDE plugin alone can't see. **Manual command only; never runs on activation or in the watcher loop.** Off by default.

statewave.github.repo.markdownDescription: Optional `owner/name` override. Leave empty to derive from the workspace's git remote (only `github.com` remotes are auto-detected).

statewave.github.token.markdownDescription: Optional GitHub Personal Access Token, **fallback only**. The preferred path is VS Code's built-in `github` authentication session (used automatically; no token typed/committed). Use this setting only when that's unavailable (Cursor, headless) — and **prefer User/Machine settings, never committed workspace settings**. Public repos work with no token (lower rate limits).

statewave.github.include.markdownDescription: Which GitHub event groups to pull. Trim this to scope the sync.

statewave.github.since.markdownDescription: ISO date (e.g. `2026-01-01T00:00:00Z`) — only events at/after this. Empty = use the last successful sync cursor (or the connector default).

statewave.github.maxItems.markdownDescription: Hard cap of items per sync — protects against runaway pulls and rate limits.

scripts.preview-release: pnpm --filter statewave-ide-companion... run build && pnpm run lint && pnpm run typecheck && pnpm run test && pnpm run package && pnpm run leak-scan
